\caption{\textbf{Exact paired within-subject MIR effects.} $\Delta$MIR $=$ \texttt{amica-python} $-$ comparator (kbits/s); positive favours \texttt{amica-python}. One row per dataset$\,\times\,$comparator contrast, reported per contrast (not averaged across comparators). 95\% confidence intervals are bootstrap ($10{,}000$ resamples); $d_z$ is the paired-difference standardized effect with bootstrap CI; $p$-values are the paired $t$-test and Wilcoxon signed-rank test. Holm$\,p$ is Holm--Bonferroni-adjusted across the family of nine MIR contrasts. ``Same-sign'' counts subjects whose contrast favours \texttt{amica-python}. Absolute MIR is not comparable across datasets (it scales with retained rank and channel geometry; sampling rate harmonised to $250$~Hz), but the within-subject contrast is.}
\label{tab:replication}
